Title: CRA Form BC428 and Worksheet BC428 2025 British Columbia resident tax.

- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/5010-d/5010-d-25e.pdf

CRA Form BC428 2025 calculates British Columbia tax for British Columbia residents. Part A calculates British Columbia tax on taxable income from line 26000. The bracket thresholds are $49,279, $98,560, $113,158, $137,407, $186,306, and $259,829. The rates are 5.06%, 7.7%, 10.5%, 12.29%, 14.7%, 16.8%, and 20.5%. The base tax amounts are $0.00, $2,493.52, $6,288.15, $7,820.94, $10,801.15, $17,989.30, and $30,341.16.

Part B calculates British Columbia non-refundable tax credits. Line 58040 basic personal amount is $12,932. Worksheet BC428 line 58080 age amount has a maximum of $5,799, starts reducing above $43,169, is zero at $81,829 or more, and uses a 15% reduction rate. Lines 58120 and 58160 use a base amount of $12,181 and a maximum of $11,073 for the spouse or common-law partner amount and eligible dependant amount after subtracting the spouse or dependant net income. Line 58175 is the British Columbia caregiver amount. Line 58360 pension income amount is capped at $1,000. Line 58440 disability amount for self is $9,699 unless the under-18 worksheet applies. Line 58689 medical expenses are reduced by the lesser of $2,689 and 3% of net income, then medical expenses for other dependants line 58729 are added. Line 58840 multiplies the total claim amount by 5.06%. Worksheet BC428 line 58969 calculates donations and gifts: the first $200 is multiplied by 5.06%, donations above $200 are split between the amount supported by taxable income above $259,829 at 20.5% and the remainder at 16.8%. Line 58980 multiplies qualifying farmers' food donation gifts by 25%. Line 61500 adds British Columbia non-refundable credits and farmers' food donation tax credit.

Part C calculates British Columbia tax. Line 63 adds British Columbia tax on taxable income and British Columbia tax on split income line 61510. Worksheet BC428 line 61520 calculates the British Columbia dividend tax credit by multiplying taxable dividends other than eligible dividends line 12010 by 1.96% and taxable dividends line 12000 minus line 12010 by 12%. Line 61540 multiplies federal minimum tax carryover line 40427 by 34.9%. Lines 67 and 68 subtract non-refundable credits, dividend tax credit, and minimum tax carryover from tax and floor negative amounts at zero. Line 69 multiplies Form T691 Part 7 line 14 by 34.9%. Line 72 subtracts provincial foreign tax credit. Lines 73 to 79 calculate the British Columbia tax reduction: if net income is $40,807 or more the reduction is zero; otherwise the basic reduction is $562 minus 3.56% of net income above $25,020, floored at zero. Lines 80 to 82 subtract the tax reduction and British Columbia logging tax credit. Line 84 calculates the British Columbia political contribution tax credit: contributions over $1,150 receive $500; otherwise the first $100 receives 75%, the next range to $550 receives 50% plus $75, and amounts over $550 receive 33.33% plus $300. Lines 85 to 91 subtract the political contribution credit, total ESOP and EVCC credits capped at $2,000, and British Columbia mining flow-through share tax credit. Line 91 enters British Columbia tax on line 42800 of the T1 return. Normalized numeric values encoded from BC428 and Worksheet BC428 include 49279, 98560, 113158, 137407, 186306, 259829, 0.0506, 0.077, 0.105, 0.1229, 0.147, 0.168, 0.205, 2493.52, 6288.15, 7820.94, 10801.15, 17989.30, 30341.16, 12932, 5799, 43169, 81829, 0.15, 12181, 11073, 1000, 9699, 2689, 0.03, 200, 40807, 562, 25020, 0.0356, 0.349, 0.0196, 0.12, 0.25, 100, 550, 1150, 500, 0.75, 0.50, 0.3333, 75, 300, and 2000.
